text: All praise is for Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And praise be to Him in the Hereafter. He is the All-Wise, All-Aware.
He knows whatever goes into the earth and whatever comes out of it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it. And He is the Most Merciful, All-Forgiving.

The disbelievers say, ‚ÄúThe Hour will never come to us.‚ÄĚ Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúYes‚ÄĒby my Lord, the Knower of the unseen‚ÄĒit will certainly come to you!‚ÄĚ Not ňĻevenňļ an atom‚Äôs weight is hidden from Him in the heavens or the earth; nor anything smaller or larger than that, but is ňĻwrittenňļ in a perfect Record. <sup foot_note=877>877</sup>
So He may reward those who believe and do good. It is they who will have forgiveness and an honourable provision.
As for those who strive to discredit Our revelations, it is they who will suffer the ňĻworstňļ torment of agonizing pain.
Those gifted with knowledge ňĻclearlyňļ see that what has been revealed to you from your Lord ňĻO Prophetňļ is the truth, and that it guides to the Path of the Almighty, the Praiseworthy.
The disbelievers say ňĻmockingly to one anotherňļ, ‚ÄúShall we show you a man who claims that when you have been utterly disintegrated you will be raised as a new creation?
Has he fabricated a lie against Allah or is he insane?‚ÄĚ In fact, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are bound for torment and have strayed farthest ňĻfrom the truthňļ.

Have they not then seen all that surrounds them of the heavens and the earth? If We willed, We could cause the earth to swallow them up, or cause ňĻdeadlyňļ pieces of the sky to fall upon them. Surely in this is a sign for every servant who turns ňĻto Allahňļ.
Indeed, We granted David a ňĻgreatňļ privilege from Us, ňĻcommanding:ňļ ‚ÄúO mountains! Echo his hymns! And the birds as well.‚ÄĚ We made iron mouldable for him,
instructing: ‚ÄúMake full-length armour, ňĻperfectlyňļ balancing the links. And work righteousness ňĻO family of David!ňļ. Indeed, I am All-Seeing of what you do.‚ÄĚ

And to Solomon ňĻWe subjectedňļ the wind: its morning stride was a month‚Äôs journey and so was its evening stride. And We caused a stream of molten copper to flow for him, and ňĻWe subjectedňļ some of the jinn to work under him by his Lord‚Äôs Will. And whoever of them deviated from Our command, We made them taste the torment of the blaze.

They made for him whatever he desired of sanctuaries, statues, <sup foot_note=878>878</sup> basins as large as reservoirs, and cooking pots fixed ňĻinto the groundňļ. ňĻWe ordered:ňļ ‚ÄúWork gratefully, O family of David!‚ÄĚ ňĻOnlyňļ a few of My servants are ňĻtrulyňļ grateful.

foot_notes.878: Which was permissible at the time of Solomon ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: When We decreed Solomon‚Äôs death, nothing indicated to the ňĻsubjectedňļ jinn that he was dead except the termites eating away his staff. <sup foot_note=879>879</sup> So when he collapsed, the jinn realized that if they had ňĻreallyňļ known the unseen, they would not have remained in ňĻsuchňļ humiliating servitude.

foot_notes.879: Which he died while leaning on.

text: Indeed, there was a sign for ňĻthe tribe ofňļ Sheba in their homeland: two orchards‚ÄĒone to the right and the other to the left. ňĻThey were told:ňļ ‚ÄúEat from the provision of your Lord, and be grateful to Him. ňĻYours isňļ a good land and a forgiving Lord.‚ÄĚ

But they turned away. So We sent against them a devastating flood, and replaced their orchards with two others producing bitter fruit, fruitless bushes, <sup foot_note=880>880</sup> and a few ňĻsparseňļ thorny trees. <sup foot_note=881>881</sup>
This is how We rewarded them for their ingratitude. Would We ever punish ňĻanyone in such a wayňļ except the ungrateful?

We had also placed between them and the cities <sup foot_note=882>882</sup> We showered with blessings ňĻmany smallňļ towns within sight of one another. And We set moderate travel distances in between, ňĻsaying,ňļ ‚ÄúTravel between them by day and night safely.‚ÄĚ

But they said, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Make ňĻthe distances ofňļ our journeys longer,‚ÄĚ wronging themselves. <sup foot_note=883>883</sup> So We reduced them to ňĻcautionaryňļ tales, and scattered them utterly. Surely in this are lessons for whoever is steadfast, grateful.

foot_notes.883: They became bored of living comfortably and traveling easily. A similar example can be found in 2:61, where the Children of Israel became bored with the manna and quails and wanted to eat other things such as onions and garlic.

text: Indeed, Ibl√ģs ‚Äô assumption about them has come true, <sup foot_note=884>884</sup> so they ňĻallňļ follow him, except a group of ňĻtrueňļ believers.

foot_notes.884: Ibl√ģs was the name of Satan before his fall from grace ( see footnote for 2:34). His assumption (or vow) was that he could easily mislead the majority of people ( see 7:16-17, 15:39-40, and 38:82-83).

text: He does not have any authority over them, but ňĻOur Will isňļ only to distinguish those who believe in the Hereafter from those who are in doubt about it. And your Lord is a ňĻvigilantňļ Keeper over all things.‚ÄĚ

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCall upon those you claim ňĻto be divineňļ besides Allah. They do not possess ňĻevenňļ an atom‚Äôs weight either in the heavens or the earth, nor do they have any share in ňĻgoverningňļ them. Nor is any of them a helper to Him.‚ÄĚ

No intercession will be of any benefit with Him, except by those granted permission by Him. ňĻAt last,ňļ when the dread ňĻof Judgment Dayňļ is relieved from their hearts ňĻbecause they are permitted to intercedeňļ, they will ňĻexcitedlyňļ ask ňĻthe angelsňļ, ‚ÄúWhat has your Lord ňĻjustňļ said?‚ÄĚ The angels will reply, ‚ÄúThe truth! And He is the Most High, All-Great.‚ÄĚ

Ask ňĻthem, O Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWho provides for you from the heavens and the earth?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúAllah! Now, certainly one of our two groups <sup foot_note=885>885</sup> is ňĻrightlyňļ guided; the other is clearly astray.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúYou will not be accountable for our misdeeds, nor will we be accountable for your deeds.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúOur Lord will gather us together, then He will judge between us with the truth. For He is the All-Knowing Judge.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúShow me those ňĻidolsňļ you have joined with Him as partners. No! In fact, He ňĻaloneňļ is Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.‚ÄĚ
We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner to all of humanity, but most people do not know.
And they ask ňĻthe believersňļ, ‚ÄúWhen will this threat come to pass, if what you say is true?‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúA Day has ňĻalreadyňļ been appointed for you, which you can neither delay nor advance by a ňĻsingleňļ moment.‚ÄĚ

The disbelievers vow, ‚ÄúWe will never believe in this Quran, nor in those ňĻScripturesňļ before it.‚ÄĚ If only you could see when the wrongdoers will be detained before their Lord, throwing blame at each other! The lowly ňĻfollowersňļ will say to the arrogant ňĻleadersňļ, ‚ÄúHad it not been for you, we would certainly have been believers.‚ÄĚ
The arrogant will respond to the lowly, ‚ÄúDid we ever hinder you from guidance after it came to you? In fact, you were wicked ňĻon your ownňļ.‚ÄĚ

The lowly will say to the arrogant, ‚ÄúNo! It was your plotting by day and night‚ÄĒwhen you ordered us to disbelieve in Allah and to set up equals with Him.‚ÄĚ They will ňĻallňļ hide ňĻtheirňļ remorse when they see the torment. And We will put shackles around the necks of the disbelievers. Will they be rewarded except for what they used to do?
Whenever We sent a warner to a society, its elite would say, ‚ÄúWe truly reject what you have been sent with.‚ÄĚ
Adding, ‚ÄúWe are far superior ňĻto the believersňļ in wealth and children, and we will never be punished.‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely ňĻit isňļ my Lord ňĻWhoňļ gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. But most people do not know.‚ÄĚ

It is not your wealth or children that bring you closer to Us. But those who believe and do good‚ÄĒit is they who will have a multiplied reward for what they did, and they will be secure in ňĻelevatedňļ mansions.
As for those who strive to discredit Our revelations, it is they who will be confined in punishment.

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúSurely ňĻit isňļ my Lord ňĻWhoňļ gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills of His servants. And whatever you spend in charity, He will compensate ňĻyouňļ for it. For He is the Best Provider.‚ÄĚ
And ňĻconsiderňļ the Day He will gather them all together, and then ask the angels, ‚ÄúWas it you that these ňĻpolytheistsňļ used to worship?‚ÄĚ
They will say, ‚ÄúGlory be to You! Our loyalty is to You, not them. In fact, they ňĻonlyňļ followed the ňĻtemptations of evilňļ jinn, <sup foot_note=886>886</sup> in whom most of them had faith.‚ÄĚ
So Today neither of you can benefit or protect each other. And We will say to the wrongdoers, ‚ÄúTaste the torment of the Fire, which you used to deny.‚ÄĚ

When Our clear revelations are recited to them, they say, ‚ÄúThis is only a man who wishes to hinder you from what your forefathers used to worship.‚ÄĚ They also say, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is no more than a fabricated lie.‚ÄĚ And the disbelievers say of the truth when it has come to them, ‚ÄúThis is nothing but pure magic.‚ÄĚ
ňĻThey say so even thoughňļ We had never given them any scriptures to study, nor did We ever send them a warner before you ňĻO Prophetňļ.

Those ňĻdestroyedňļ before them denied as well‚ÄĒand these ňĻMeccansňļ have not attained even one-tenth of what We had given their predecessors. <sup foot_note=887>887</sup> Yet ňĻwhenňļ they denied My messengers, how severe was My response!

foot_notes.887: Like the people of Pharaoh, ‚Äô»ād, and Tham√Ľd.

text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI advise you to do ňĻonlyňļ one thing: stand up for ňĻthe sake ofňļ Allah‚ÄĒindividually or in pairs‚ÄĒthen reflect. Your fellow man <sup foot_note=888>888</sup> is not insane. He is only a warner to you before ňĻthe coming ofňļ a severe punishment.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúIf I had ever asked you for a reward, you could keep it. My reward is only from Allah. And He is a Witness over all things.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúSurely my Lord hurls the truth ňĻagainst falsehoodňļ. ňĻHe isňļ the Knower of all unseen.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúThe truth has come, and falsehood will vanish, never to return.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúIf I am astray, the loss is only mine. And if I am guided, it is ňĻonlyňļ because of what my Lord reveals to me. He is indeed All-Hearing, Ever Near.‚ÄĚ
If only you could see when they will be horrified with no escape ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ! And they will be seized from a nearby place. <sup foot_note=889>889</sup>

foot_notes.889: From the place of Judgment to Hell.

text: They will ňĻthenňļ cry, ‚ÄúWe do ňĻnowňļ believe in it ňĻallňļ.‚ÄĚ But how could they ňĻpossiblyňļ attain faith from a place so far-off ňĻfrom the worldňļ, <sup foot_note=890>890</sup>

foot_notes.890: When it is already too late.

text: while they had already rejected it before, guessing blindly from a place ňĻequallyňļ far-away ňĻfrom the Hereafterňļ?
They will be sealed off from whatever they desire, as was done to their counterparts before. Indeed, they were ňĻallňļ in alarming doubt.
